extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL attacked on the final climb with 2km to go, dropped ALAPHILIPPE and BERNAL, won solo by 5 seconds.
tactical_patterns: VAN DER POEL launched multiple accelerations before final decisive move, testing ALAPHILIPPE and BERNAL's reserves before terminal attack.
Short 800m gravel sector splintered field decisively, proving brief intense sectors matter more than total gravel kilometers.
Three-up group cooperated into finale despite knowing VAN DER POEL's power advantage, allowing him to recover between surges.

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: maintained power edge after repeated accelerations, demonstrating superior repeatability over one-off climbers in finale.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: closed gaps to VAN DER POEL multiple times but lacked final punch when decisive acceleration came.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: dropped before ALAPHILIPPE despite following wheels, suggesting gravel handling deficit compounds climbing on unstable surfaces.
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: finished sixth in group behind podium, marking strong debut adaptation to gravel despite no prior experience.
rider_signals.CLARKE Simon: positive: finished eighth leading chase group, rare top-10 for pure domestique suggesting gravel rewards diesel engines.
